channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ABC Sydney broadcaster and musician James Valentine remembered for decades of influential radio work
item.description: James Valentine, a much-loved ABC Sydney radio presenter and accomplished musician, has died at the age of 64. Known for his warm, inclusive style and thoughtful curiosity, Valentine spent more than t...

item.title: Api Koroisau faces possible suspension after two dangerous tackles
item.description: Wests Tigers hooker Api Koroisau could be facing disciplinary action following a hip-drop tackle on Canberra's Noah Martin and a lifting tackle on Morgan Smithies during Thursday night's NRL clash at ...

item.title: Alex Eala and Zeynep Sonmez reach Madrid Open doubles Round of 16
item.description: Maganda ang takbo ng kampanya ni Filipina tennis player Alex Eala sa 2026 Madrid Open matapos siyang umabante hindi lang sa singles kundi pati sa doubles event. Kasama ang kanyang ka-partner na si Zey...

item.title: Ontario’s Upcoming HST Rebate: What Homebuyers Should Know
item.description: Ontario’s government has announced a one-year HST rebate for new home purchases, aiming to help families achieve homeownership and stimulate construction. The rebate, included in the 2026 budget, appl...

item.title: 20 Chinese nationals arrested for alleged illegal gold and copper mining in Davao Region
item.description: Authorities in the Philippines arrested 20 Chinese nationals in separate operations in the Davao Region for their alleged involvement in illegal gold and copper mining activities. The joint operations...

item.title: Several Injured After Two Trains Collide Near Copenhagen
item.description: On Thursday, a serious train accident occurred near Copenhagen, Denmark, when two trains collided head-on at Gribskov, around 40 kilometres north of the capital. According to media and police reports,...

item.title: Trump Orders U.S. Navy to Respond Aggressively to Iranian Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tensions in the Strait of Hormuz have escalated after President Donald Trump issued a stark warning, instructing the U.S. Navy to 'shoot and kill' any Iranian boats laying mines in the critical waterw...

item.title: Elections in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu Begin Amid Security and Political Tensions
item.description: Voting has started in two politically significant Indian states, West Bengal and Tamil Nadu, with millions of citizens participating in the electoral process. In West Bengal, the first phase covers 15...

item.title: US government reclassifies marijuana for better access to medical use
item.description: For Thursday, April 23, 2026, the US government don announce say marijuana go dey easier for people wey need am for medical use. This new decision follow after the US authorities change the classifica...

item.title: Senate approves Muttaqha Rabe Darma as minister after screening session
item.description: The Nigerian Senate on Thursday has confirmed the appointment of Dr. Muttaqha Rabe Darma as a Minister of the Federal Republic of Nigeria following a formal request from President Bola Tinubu. The con...

item.title: Rising Prices of Fresh Vegetables Driven by Cucumber Costs and Supply Shortages
item.description: In March 2026, Statistics Canada reported a 7.8% year-over-year increase in fresh vegetable prices, with cucumbers seeing a sharp rise of 28.4%. Consumers, especially those looking to eat more vegetab...

item.title: Kenya Engages Italian Firms to Expand Investment in Key Sectors
item.description: Kenya is stepping up efforts to attract foreign direct investment from Italy through active engagement at the Italy-Kenya Economic Forum in Rome. The event has brought together over 130 Italian compan...

item.title: Kenya Faces Funding Shortfall in Free Education, MPs Suggest Subsidised Model
item.description: Kenya's lawmakers are now urging a formal shift in the country's education policy after the government admitted it cannot fully fund the free education programme. Over 1.1 million secondary school stu...

item.title: FBR revises customs valuation upward for 62 used mobile phone models to match market rates
item.description: The Federal Board of Revenue (FBR) through its Directorate General of Customs Valuation has revised and increased the customs valuation for 62 models of old and used mobile phones in Pakistan. This up...

item.title: Sasol sees increased demand for fuel and chemicals as global oil supply tightens
item.description: Sasol has reported a significant surge in demand for its oil products and chemicals amidst growing global tensions, particularly in the Middle East, which have led to disruptions in oil supply. This d...

item.title: Three migrants convicted for gang rape of woman on Brighton beach
item.description: Three asylum seekers have been convicted of gang raping a woman on Brighton beach in October 2025. Iranian-born Abdulla Ahmadi, 26, and Egyptians Karin Al-Danasurt, 20, and Ibrahim Alshafe, 25, attack...

item.title: Maimane challenges ANC over dismissal with legal action
item.description: Douglas Maimane, the recently dismissed mayor of Madibeng, has served the ANC with a lawyer's letter demanding an explanation for his removal. The ANC's secretary-general, Fikile Mbalula, confirmed th...

item.title: Inquiry report shows 518 deaths and widespread injuries after Tanzania’s 2025 election unrest
item.description: A Commission of Inquiry into the violence that followed Tanzania’s October 29, 2025 elections has confirmed that 518 people lost their lives, with most deaths caused by gunshot wounds. The findings we...

item.title: US envoy suggests Italy as potential replacement for Iran at 2026 World Cup
item.description: A senior US envoy, Paolo Zampolli, has proposed that Italy replace Iran at the 2026 FIFA World Cup following growing geopolitical tensions surrounding Iran's participation. Zampolli, a special envoy t...

item.title: UP Board 2026 Results and Top Rank Holders List for Class 10 and 12
